passage_title: Why we need to protect polar bears

sentences.sentence: Polar bears are being increasingly threatened by the effects of climate change, but their disappearance could have far-reaching consequences.
sentences.translation: 北极熊正日益受到气候变化影响的威胁，但它们的消失可能会带来深远后果。

structure.text: Polar bears are being increasingly threatened by the effects of climate change
but their disappearance could have far-reaching consequences

difficult_points: are being threatened 是现在进行时的被动结构，强调威胁正在加剧。
by the effects of climate change 说明威胁的来源。
but 引出比“受到威胁”更进一步的结果：消失会造成深远影响。

sentences.sentence: They are uniquely adapted to the extreme conditions of the Arctic Circle, where temperatures can reach −40°C.
sentences.translation: 它们独特地适应了北极圈的极端环境，那里气温可低至零下40摄氏度。

structure.text: the extreme conditions of the Arctic Circle

difficult_points: They 指 polar bears。
be adapted to 表示生理或行为上适应某种环境。
where 引导非限制性定语从句，补充说明 Arctic Circle 的温度条件。

sentences.sentence: One reason for this is that they have up to 11 centimetres of fat underneath their skin.
sentences.translation: 原因之一是它们皮下有厚达11厘米的脂肪。

structure.text: that they have up to 11 centimetres of fat underneath their skin

difficult_points: this 指上一句所说北极熊能适应极端寒冷环境。
that 引导表语从句，解释 one reason 的具体内容。
up to 表示最高可达到的程度。

expressions.text: One reason for this is that

sentences.sentence: Humans with comparative levels of adipose tissue would be considered obese and would be likely to suffer from diabetes and heart disease.
sentences.translation: 拥有相近水平脂肪组织的人类会被视为肥胖，并且很可能患上糖尿病和心脏病。

structure.text: Humans with comparative levels of adipose tissue
and would be likely to suffer from diabetes and heart disease
to suffer from diabetes and heart disease

difficult_points: with comparative levels of adipose tissue 后置修饰 Humans。
would be considered 与 would be likely 是假设性表达，用人类作对比。
suffer from 后接疾病，表示患有某病。

sentences.sentence: Yet the polar bear experiences no such consequences.
sentences.translation: 然而，北极熊并不会经历这样的后果。
difficult_points: Yet 表示与人类情况形成转折。
no such consequences 指前一句的肥胖、糖尿病和心脏病等后果。
experiences 在这里表示“经历、遭受”，不是实验意义。

sentences.sentence: A 2014 study by Shi Ping Liu and colleagues sheds light on this mystery.
sentences.translation: 石平刘及其同事在2014年的一项研究揭示了这一谜团。

structure.text: A 2014 study by Shi Ping Liu and colleagues

difficult_points: by Shi Ping Liu and colleagues 说明研究的作者。
sheds light on 是固定表达，表示帮助解释或揭示。
this mystery 指北极熊脂肪多却不出现人类相关疾病的问题。

sentences.sentence: They compared the genetic structure of polar bears with that of their closest relatives from a warmer climate, the brown bears.
sentences.translation: 他们把北极熊的基因结构与其来自较温暖气候地区的最近亲缘物种——棕熊——的基因结构进行了比较。

structure.text: They compared the genetic structure of polar bears
with that of their closest relatives from a warmer climate

difficult_points: They 指研究者 Shi Ping Liu and colleagues。
that 代替 genetic structure，避免重复。
the brown bears 是 their closest relatives 的同位语。

sentences.sentence: This allowed them to determine the genes that have allowed polar bears to survive in one of the toughest environments on Earth.
sentences.translation: 这使他们能够确定哪些基因让北极熊得以在地球上最严酷的环境之一中生存。

structure.text: This allowed them to determine the genes
that have allowed polar bears to survive in one of the toughest environments on Earth
to survive in one of the toughest environments on Earth

difficult_points: This 指前一句的基因结构比较。
that 引导定语从句，修饰 genes。
one of the toughest environments on Earth 强调北极环境的严酷程度。

sentences.sentence: Liu and his colleagues found the polar bears had a gene known as ApoB, which reduces levels of low-density lipoproteins (LDLs) – a form of 'bad' cholesterol.
sentences.translation: 刘和他的同事发现北极熊拥有一种名为 ApoB 的基因，它会降低低密度脂蛋白（LDL）的水平，而低密度脂蛋白是一种“坏”胆固醇。

structure.text: the polar bears had a gene known as ApoB
which reduces levels of low-density lipoproteins (LDLs)

difficult_points: found 后省略 that，后面的完整句说明研究发现。
known as ApoB 是过去分词短语，修饰 a gene。
which 引导非限制性定语从句，说明 ApoB 基因的作用。

sentences.sentence: In humans, mutations of this gene are associated with increased risk of heart disease.
sentences.translation: 在人类中，这一基因的突变与心脏病风险增加有关。
difficult_points: In humans 限定这个基因突变在人类中的影响。
this gene 指上一句的 ApoB 基因。
are associated with 表示相关，并不一定直接表示因果关系。

sentences.sentence: Polar bears may therefore be an important study model to understand heart disease in humans.
sentences.translation: 因此，北极熊可能是理解人类心脏病的重要研究模型。

structure.text: to understand heart disease in humans

difficult_points: therefore 表示这一结论来自前文关于 ApoB 基因和心脏病的发现。
may 表示可能性，语气谨慎。
to understand... 说明把北极熊作为研究模型的目的。

sentences.sentence: The genome of the polar bear may also provide the solution for another condition, one that particularly affects our older generation: osteoporosis.
sentences.translation: 北极熊的基因组也可能为另一种疾病提供解决方案，这种疾病尤其影响老年一代：骨质疏松症。

structure.text: The genome of the polar bear
one that particularly affects our older generation

difficult_points: may also provide 表示除心脏病外还可能带来另一类医学启示。
one 指 another condition，并由 that 从句进一步说明。
冒号后的 osteoporosis 给出这种疾病的具体名称。

sentences.sentence: This is a disease where bones show reduced density, usually caused by insufficient exercise, reduced calcium intake or food starvation.
sentences.translation: 这是一种骨骼密度降低的疾病，通常由运动不足、钙摄入减少或食物匮乏造成。

structure.text: a disease where bones show reduced density
usually caused by insufficient exercise, reduced calcium intake or food starvation

difficult_points: This 指上一句的 osteoporosis。
where 引导定语从句，说明这种疾病的表现。
usually caused by... 是过去分词短语，说明常见原因。

sentences.sentence: Bone tissue is constantly being remodelled, meaning that bone is added or removed, depending on nutrient availability and the stress that the bone is under.
sentences.translation: 骨组织会不断重塑，也就是说骨质会根据营养供应情况以及骨骼承受的压力被增加或移除。

structure.text: Bone tissue is constantly being remodelled
meaning that bone is added or removed
depending on nutrient availability and the stress that the bone is under

difficult_points: is being remodelled 是现在进行时被动结构，强调骨组织持续被重塑。
meaning that... 解释 remodelled 的具体含义。
depending on... 表示骨质增加或移除取决于哪些条件。

sentences.sentence: Female polar bears, however, undergo extreme conditions during every pregnancy.
sentences.translation: 然而，雌性北极熊在每次怀孕期间都会经历极端条件。
difficult_points: however 插入句中，提示雌性北极熊与一般骨质流失预期形成对比。
undergo 表示经历较艰难或显著的情况。
during every pregnancy 限定极端条件出现的阶段。

sentences.sentence: Once autumn comes around, these females will dig maternity dens in the snow and will remain there throughout the winter, both before and after the birth of their cubs.
sentences.translation: 一到秋天，这些雌熊就会在雪中挖产仔洞，并在整个冬天都留在那里，幼崽出生前后都是如此。

structure.text: these females will dig maternity dens in the snow
and will remain there throughout the winter
both before and after the birth of their cubs

difficult_points: Once 引导时间状语从句，表示秋天到来后发生的行为。
dig 与 remain 是 these females 的并列动作。
both before and after... 说明雌熊留在洞中的时间覆盖幼崽出生前后。

sentences.sentence: This process results in about six months of fasting, where the female bears have to keep themselves and their cubs alive, depleting their own calcium and calorie reserves.
sentences.translation: 这一过程导致大约六个月的禁食，在此期间雌熊必须维持自己和幼崽的生命，从而消耗自身的钙和热量储备。

structure.text: This process results in about six months of fasting
where the female bears have to keep themselves and their cubs alive
to keep themselves and their cubs alive
depleting their own calcium and calorie reserves

difficult_points: This process 指前一句挖洞并留在雪洞中过冬的过程。
where 在这里指 fasting 这一阶段，而不是地点。
depleting... 是结果性现在分词短语，说明禁食带来的消耗。

sentences.sentence: Despite this, their bones remain strong and dense.
sentences.translation: 尽管如此，它们的骨骼仍然强健而致密。
difficult_points: Despite this 指尽管经历长期禁食和储备消耗。
remain 是系动词，表示状态保持不变。
strong and dense 与骨质疏松的 reduced density 形成对比。

sentences.sentence: Physiologists Alanda Lennox and Allen Goodship found an explanation for this paradox in 2008.
sentences.translation: 生理学家阿兰达·伦诺克斯和艾伦·古德希普在2008年为这一悖论找到了一个解释。

structure.text: Physiologists Alanda Lennox and Allen Goodship

difficult_points: Physiologists 说明两位研究者的学科身份。
this paradox 指雌性北极熊长期禁食却骨骼仍强健的矛盾现象。
an explanation for... 表示对某问题的解释。

sentences.sentence: They discovered that pregnant bears were able to increase the density of their bones before they started to build their dens.
sentences.translation: 他们发现，怀孕的熊能够在开始筑洞之前提高骨骼密度。

structure.text: that pregnant bears were able to increase the density of their bones before they started to build their dens
were able to increase the density of their bones
before they started to build their dens

difficult_points: They 指上一句的两位生理学家。
that 引导宾语从句，说明研究发现。
before 引导时间从句，强调骨密度增加发生在筑洞之前。

sentences.sentence: In addition, six months later, when they finally emerged from the den with their cubs, there was no evidence of significant loss of bone density.
sentences.translation: 此外，六个月后，当它们终于带着幼崽从洞穴中出来时，没有证据显示骨密度出现显著流失。

structure.text: when they finally emerged from the den with their cubs
there was no evidence of significant loss of bone density

difficult_points: In addition 表示在前一项发现之外还有新的观察。
when 从句说明检查骨密度的时间点。
no evidence of significant loss 表示没有明显流失的证据。

sentences.sentence: Hibernating brown bears do not have this capacity and must therefore resort to major bone reformation in the following spring.
sentences.translation: 冬眠的棕熊没有这种能力，因此必须在第二年春天进行大规模骨骼重建。

structure.text: and must therefore resort to major bone reformation in the following spring

difficult_points: Hibernating 是现在分词形式的形容词，修饰 brown bears。
this capacity 指提前增加骨密度并避免显著流失的能力。
resort to 表示不得不采用某种办法。

sentences.sentence: If the mechanism of bone remodelling in polar bears can be understood, many bedridden humans, and even astronauts, could potentially benefit.
sentences.translation: 如果北极熊骨骼重塑的机制能够被理解，许多卧床不起的人，甚至宇航员，都可能从中受益。

structure.text: If the mechanism of bone remodelling in polar bears can be understood
many bedridden humans, and even astronauts,

difficult_points: If 引导条件从句，提出医学应用的前提。
can be understood 是被动结构，表示机制被研究清楚。
and even astronauts 是插入式补充，强调受益群体不止卧床者。

sentences.sentence: The medical benefits of the polar bear for humanity certainly have their importance in our conservation efforts, but these should not be the only factors taken into consideration.
sentences.translation: 北极熊对人类的医学益处在我们的保护努力中当然很重要，但这些不应是唯一被考虑的因素。

structure.text: The medical benefits of the polar bear for humanity certainly have their importance in our conservation efforts
but these should not be the only factors taken into consideration

difficult_points: The medical benefits...for humanity 是主语，核心词是 benefits。
these 指医学益处。
but 引出作者观点：保护北极熊不能只看医学价值。

sentences.sentence: We tend to want to protect animals we think are intelligent and possess emotions, such as elephants and primates.
sentences.translation: 我们往往想保护那些我们认为聪明且拥有情感的动物，比如大象和灵长类动物。

structure.text: We tend to want to protect animals
we think are intelligent and possess emotions

difficult_points: tend to 表示一种普遍倾向。
we think... 是省略关系词的定语从句，修饰 animals。
such as 后面举例说明这类动物。

sentences.sentence: Bears, on the other hand, seem to be perceived as stupid and in many cases violent.
sentences.translation: 另一方面，熊似乎被认为愚笨，而且在许多情况下很暴力。

structure.text: stupid and in many cases violent

difficult_points: on the other hand 表示与前文大象和灵长类动物形成对照。
seem to be perceived as 是被动感知结构，表示外界对熊的看法。
in many cases 修饰 violent，表示并非所有情况。

sentences.sentence: And yet anecdotal evidence from the field challenges those assumptions, suggesting for example that polar bears have good problem-solving abilities.
sentences.translation: 然而，来自野外的轶事证据挑战了这些假设，例如表明北极熊具有良好的解决问题能力。

structure.text: And yet anecdotal evidence from the field challenges those assumptions
suggesting for example that polar bears have good problem-solving abilities
that polar bears have good problem-solving abilities

difficult_points: And yet 表示强烈转折，反驳前面对熊的刻板印象。
those assumptions 指熊愚笨、暴力的看法。
suggesting... 是现在分词短语，说明证据挑战假设的具体内容。

sentences.sentence: A male bear called GoGo in Tennoji Zoo, Osaka, has even been observed making use of a tool to manipulate his environment.
sentences.translation: 大阪天王寺动物园一只名叫 GoGo 的雄性熊甚至被观察到会使用工具来操控自己的环境。

structure.text: A male bear called GoGo in Tennoji Zoo, Osaka,
making use of a tool to manipulate his environment

difficult_points: called GoGo 是过去分词短语，修饰 A male bear。
has been observed 是现在完成时被动结构，表示已有观察记录。
making use of a tool 说明被观察到的具体行为。

sentences.sentence: The bear used a tree branch on multiple occasions to dislodge a piece of meat hung out of his reach.
sentences.translation: 这只熊多次使用树枝，把一块挂在够不到位置的肉弄下来。

structure.text: to dislodge a piece of meat hung out of his reach

difficult_points: The bear 指上一句的 GoGo。
on multiple occasions 强调这种行为不止一次发生。
hung out of his reach 后置修饰 a piece of meat，说明肉的位置。

sentences.sentence: Problem-solving ability has also been witnessed in wild polar bears, although not as obviously as with GoGo.
sentences.translation: 在野生北极熊身上也观察到了解决问题的能力，虽然不像 GoGo 那样明显。

structure.text: although not as obviously as with GoGo

difficult_points: has been witnessed 是现在完成时被动结构，表示已有目击或观察记录。
also 表示野外北极熊也有类似能力。
although 引导让步结构，说明明显程度不如 GoGo。

sentences.sentence: A calculated move by a male bear involved running and jumping onto barrels in an attempt to get to a photographer standing on a platform four metres high.
sentences.translation: 一只雄性熊的一次经过计算的行动包括奔跑并跳上桶，试图接近一名站在四米高平台上的摄影师。

structure.text: A calculated move by a male bear
in an attempt to get to a photographer standing on a platform four metres high
standing on a platform four metres high

difficult_points: calculated move 表示有计划、有目的的行动。
running and jumping 是并列动名词，作 involved 的宾语。
standing... 后置修饰 photographer，说明摄影师所在位置。

sentences.sentence: In other studies, such as one by Alison Ames in 2008, polar bears showed deliberate and focused manipulation.
sentences.translation: 在其他研究中，例如艾莉森·艾姆斯2008年的一项研究，北极熊表现出有意识且专注的操控行为。

structure.text: such as one by Alison Ames in 2008

difficult_points: such as one by Alison Ames in 2008 是插入式例子，说明 other studies。
deliberate and focused 都修饰 manipulation，强调行为不是随机的。
showed 在这里表示研究观察到了某种能力。

sentences.sentence: For example, Ames observed bears putting objects in piles and then knocking them over in what appeared to be a game.
sentences.translation: 例如，艾姆斯观察到熊把物体堆成堆，然后把它们撞倒，这看起来像是一种游戏。

structure.text: Ames observed bears putting objects in piles and then knocking them over
putting objects in piles and then knocking them over
in what appeared to be a game

difficult_points: observed bears putting... 是“观察到某对象正在做某事”的结构。
them 指前面的 objects。
what appeared to be a game 表示这种行为看起来像游戏，但语气仍保留观察判断。

sentences.sentence: The study demonstrates that bears are capable of agile and thought-out behaviours.
sentences.translation: 这项研究表明，熊能够表现出灵活且经过思考的行为。

structure.text: that bears are capable of agile and thought-out behaviours

difficult_points: demonstrates 后接 that 从句，说明研究证明的内容。
be capable of 后接名词或动名词，表示有能力做某事。
thought-out 表示经过考虑的，而不是本能或偶然行为。

sentences.sentence: These examples suggest bears have greater creativity and problem-solving abilities than previously thought.
sentences.translation: 这些例子表明，熊的创造力和解决问题能力比过去认为的更强。

structure.text: bears have greater creativity and problem-solving abilities than previously thought

difficult_points: suggest 后省略 that，后面是这些例子支持的结论。
greater...than... 是比较结构。
previously thought 是省略结构，可理解为 than was previously thought。

expressions.meaning_zh: 创造力和解决问题的能力

sentences.sentence: As for emotions, while the evidence is once again anecdotal, many bears have been seen to hit out at ice and snow – seemingly out of frustration – when they have just missed out on a kill.
sentences.translation: 至于情感，虽然证据再次只是轶事性的，但许多熊被看到在刚刚错失猎物时拍打冰雪——看起来是出于沮丧。

structure.text: while the evidence is once again anecdotal
many bears have been seen to hit out at ice and snow
when they have just missed out on a kill

difficult_points: As for emotions 转入讨论北极熊是否有情感。
while 引导让步从句，承认证据仍属轶事性质。
when 从句说明拍打冰雪发生在刚错失猎物之后。

sentences.sentence: Moreover, polar bears can form unusual relationships with other species, including playing with the dogs used to pull sleds in the Arctic.
sentences.translation: 此外，北极熊还能与其他物种形成不同寻常的关系，包括与北极地区用来拉雪橇的狗玩耍。

structure.text: polar bears can form unusual relationships with other species
including playing with the dogs used to pull sleds in the Arctic
used to pull sleds in the Arctic

difficult_points: Moreover 表示在情感证据之外再增加一类行为证据。
including playing... 列举 unusual relationships 的具体例子。
used to pull sleds in the Arctic 后置修饰 dogs。

expressions.meaning_zh: 与……形成不同寻常的关系

sentences.sentence: Remarkably, one hand-raised polar bear called Agee has formed a close relationship with her owner Mark Dumas to the point where they even swim together.
sentences.translation: 令人惊讶的是，一只名叫 Agee、由人类抚养大的北极熊与其主人马克·杜马斯建立了亲密关系，以至于他们甚至会一起游泳。

structure.text: one hand-raised polar bear called Agee
has formed a close relationship with her owner Mark Dumas
to the point where they even swim together

difficult_points: hand-raised 表示由人类抚养长大，这解释了它与人类的亲密关系。
called Agee 后置修饰 one hand-raised polar bear。
to the point where... 表示关系发展到某种程度。

sentences.sentence: This is even more astonishing since polar bears are known to actively hunt humans in the wild.
sentences.translation: 这一点更加令人惊讶，因为众所周知，野外的北极熊会主动猎杀人类。

structure.text: since polar bears are known to actively hunt humans in the wild
are known to actively hunt humans

difficult_points: This 指上一句中 Agee 与主人亲密到一起游泳的事实。
since 引导原因状语从句，解释为什么这件事更惊人。
are known to 表示“众所周知会”，后接主动行为。

sentences.sentence: If climate change were to lead to their extinction, this would mean the loss not only of potential breakthroughs in human medicine, but more importantly, the disappearance of an intelligent, majestic animal.
sentences.translation: 如果气候变化导致它们灭绝，这不仅意味着人类医学潜在突破的丧失，更重要的是，也意味着一种聪明而雄伟的动物的消失。

structure.text: If climate change were to lead to their extinction
not only of potential breakthroughs in human medicine
but more importantly, the disappearance of an intelligent, majestic animal

difficult_points: If...were to... 是较正式的假设条件，表示气候变化若最终导致灭绝。
this 指北极熊灭绝这一情况。
not only...but more importantly... 强调作者认为失去这种动物本身比医学损失更重要。
